title: Talravin

{{Quote|When I joined the Order I left my family on Talravin, as all Padawans do. My family is still there, the last that I heard.|Bastila Shan|<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Talravin''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] celestial body located on the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|hyperlane]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bastila Shan]]. More than three [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] later, it was the site of a [[Talravin casino|casino]]. A [[Unidentified Rodian thief|Rodian thief]] once broke into its vault but accidentally locked himself inside. At some point generations prior to that time, a [[Unidentified starship (Talravin)|starship]] also crashlanded on Talravin, in the process creating a large canyon.

==Description==
Talravin was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="Deep Stone Scar" /> located in the [[Talravin system/Legends|Talravin system]], a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. By [[3705 BBY/Legends|3705 BBY]], Talravin was situated between the [[Fedalle system/Legends|Fedalle]] and [[Ruul system/Legends|Ruul systems]] on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] known as the [[Brentaal–Denon Route]]. At some point following [[3694 BBY]], the hyperlane was integrated into the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:Bastila_render.png|thumb|right|220px|Talravin was the homeworld of Bastila Shan.]]
The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bastila Shan]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on Talravin<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> in the [[Shan family|family]] of the Human [[Helena Shan]] and her [[Bastila Shan's father|husband]], who was a [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunter]]. At a young age, Bastila spent a lot of time following her [[Parent/Legends|father]] on [[Starship/Legends|starships]] as part of treasure hunts. Ultimately, however, she was given up for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> By [[3959 BBY]], Talravin was a part of the "civilized" [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], or the areas that had been widely [[Scout/Legends|explored]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TEA" /> [[3956 BBY|Three years]] later,<ref name="3956 BBY">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' to [[3956 BBY]].</ref> Bastila, by then a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], told about her origin on Talravin to the Jedi trainee [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], by which time the information she had received indicated that her family still resided there. In reality, however, both of her parents had traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where Bastila's father had [[Death/Legends|perished]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

A [[Unidentified starship (Talravin)|starship]] at one point crashlanded on Talravin, in the process creating an expansive canyon. Over subsequent generations, looters [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenged]] the ship's wreckage, although a [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] case that contained what would later be highly valued antiquities remained buried underground.<ref name="Deep Stone Scar" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="Date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the missions "Deep Stone Scar" and "Sealed in Stupidity" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the game, the missions must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] discovered a [[Black box/Legends|black box]] that pointed to the true nature of the canyon Talravin. The individual then sent their companion to investigate the remains of the crashed vessel, and the latter ultimately uncovered the case with the antiquities.<ref name="Deep Stone Scar" /> Around the same time,<ref name="Date" /> a [[Unidentified Rodian thief|Rodian thief]] broke into a high-security vault of a [[Talravin casino|casino]] but accidentally locked himself inside. He then sent a [[Hologram/Legends|holotransmission]] to an individual affiliated with either the Republic or the Sith Empire, successfully petitioning them to rescue him in exchange for a portion of the stolen valuables.<ref name="Sealed in Stupidity" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
Talravin was the site of a [[Casino/Legends|casino]]<ref name="Sealed in Stupidity" /> as well as a large canyon that had at one point been created by the crashlanding of a starship.<ref name="Deep Stone Scar" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Phonebook.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Talravin was listed in a tongue-in-cheek in-universe phonebook depicted in ''Star Wars Insider'' 88.]]
Talravin was first mentioned in the [[2003]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.<ref name="KOTOR" /> In the "[[Comlink (department)|Comlink]]" department of<ref name="Insider88">{{InsiderCite|88|Comlink}}</ref> the [[June 13]], [[2006]]<ref name="Insider88Date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20060518.html|text=The Sith Rule ''Star Wars Insider'' #88}}</ref> [[Star Wars Insider 88|eighty-eighth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, a tongue-in-cheek [[in-universe]] [[Wikipedia:Telephone directory|phonebook]] was depicted. In it, an entry for an individual from Talravin,<ref name="Insider88" /> with which the ''[[Star Wars]]'' character Bastila Shan is associated,<ref name="KOTOR" /> was listed above those for persons from [[Thesme]] and [[Ophideraan]],<ref name="Insider88" /> which are in turn the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]] of the characters [[Silya Shessaun/Legends|Silya Shessaun]] and [[Tanith Shire]], respectively<ref name="DB Shessaun">{{DB|character|silyashessaun|Shessaun, Silya}}</ref><ref name="DB Shire">{{DB|character|tanithshire|Shire, Tanith}}</ref>—and whose first names are indeed partially visible in the aforementioned image.<ref name="Insider88" />

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Talravin [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Talravin itself, in grid square M-10. In the book, the map of the Core Worlds places the Talravin system on the Hydian Way [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route, while the map of the [[Jedi Civil War]] instead places it on the route known as the [[Commenor Run/Legends|Commenor Run]]. This article assumes the latter placement is erroneous.<ref name="TEA" /> Talravin was mentioned in two Treasure Hunting Crew Skill missions of the [[2011]] BioWare video game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', though the information regarding the missions' outcome was later removed from the game.<ref name="Deep Stone Scar" /><ref name="Sealed in Stupidity" />
